this_week: Continued frontline fighting in Donetsk. EU 15th sanctions package in preparation targeting metals trade. Grain corridor negotiations stalled.
skf_relevance: Lutsk plant in western Ukraine operating at reduced capacity. Russian market fully exited. Sanctions compliance ongoing for indirect supply chains.
watchpoint: Escalation to western Ukraine. New EU sanctions on steel/metals. Energy infrastructure targeting before winter.

actions.action: Maintain dual-sourcing of Lutsk production lines to Chodov (Czech) and Karnare (Bulgaria)
Ensure 4-week buffer stock of Lutsk-specific bearing SKUs at Tongeren hub
Review employee safety protocols and evacuation plans quarterly
Monitor EU 15th sanctions package for steel/metals trade implications on supplier base
Update sanctions compliance screening for Central Asian and Turkish indirect supply chains

sources: ISW (Institute for the Study of War)

this_week: New semiconductor export controls announced. PLA exercises near Taiwan increased. Diplomatic channels remain open but tense.
skf_relevance: 32 SKF sites in China. Dual-use technology scrutiny on sensor-integrated bearings. Customer relationship management across both blocs increasingly complex.
watchpoint: Taiwan contingency. Technology decoupling acceleration. Secondary sanctions risk on Chinese entities.

actions.action: Audit all sensor-integrated bearing product lines for dual-use export control classification
Develop China-for-China and West-for-West production strategies to minimize cross-bloc dependency
Map semiconductor component suppliers for sensor bearings and identify non-Chinese alternatives
Brief executive team on Taiwan contingency plan including 120K units/week exposure
Engage trade counsel on secondary sanctions risk for Chinese entity transactions

risk: Middle East Instability — Red Sea / Iran
this_week: Houthi attacks continue despite US/UK strikes. Iran nuclear talks at impasse. Israel-Gaza situation unresolved.
skf_relevance: Suez Canal and Strait of Hormuz both under elevated risk. EU-Asia shipping corridor disrupted. Dubai and Dammam sales offices monitoring regional stability.
watchpoint: Strait of Hormuz closure risk. Expansion of Red Sea conflict. Oil price spikes affecting logistics costs.

actions.action: Maintain 2-week additional safety stock at Tongeren for Asia-sourced components on Cape routing
Develop Strait of Hormuz closure contingency for Dubai and Dammam offices (employee safety + operations)
Monitor oil price trajectory for logistics cost pass-through decisions
Pre-negotiate air freight capacity for critical OEM shipments if sea lanes further degrade

this_week: Rising nationalist parties challenging industrial policy consensus. Debates on trade defense instruments intensifying. Green Deal implementation timelines under pressure.
skf_relevance: EU is SKF's largest market. Industrial policy changes affect steel safeguards, anti-dumping duties, and carbon border adjustments — all directly impacting bearing manufacturing costs.
watchpoint: EU elections aftermath. Trade defense instrument revisions. CBAM implementation timeline.

actions.action: Track CBAM implementation timeline for steel and aluminum input cost impacts
Engage EU trade defense instrument consultations to protect bearing anti-dumping duties
Model cost scenarios for different steel safeguard quota outcomes in 2026 review
Prepare briefing for Board on EU industrial policy shift implications for SKF's EU manufacturing footprint

this_week: Continued military deployments at LAC. Bilateral trade paradoxically growing. Some diplomatic thaw in business relations.
skf_relevance: SKF has 5 factories in India and 7+ in China. Component flows between the two countries minimal but customer relationships span both. India positioning as China+1 alternative.
watchpoint: LAC escalation. India trade restrictions on Chinese imports. Supply chain China+1 acceleration.

actions.action: Accelerate India capacity expansion at Pune-Chakan for China+1 bearing production serving APAC and EU
Minimize India-China cross-border component dependencies in production planning
Monitor Indian government restrictions on Chinese imports for bearing raw materials
Develop dual customer relationship strategies for accounts active in both India and China

this_week: European PMI showing tentative recovery. US manufacturing orders stabilizing. China stimulus measures showing some effect on industrial output.
skf_relevance: Bearing demand is a direct proxy for industrial activity. Recovery in EU/US manufacturing benefits all SKF segments. China construction sector remains weak affecting infrastructure bearings.
watchpoint: EU PMI trend confirmation. China property sector. US interest rate trajectory and capex investment cycle.

actions.action: Track monthly PMI data for EU, US, and China to calibrate production planning
Prepare demand ramp-up scenarios for EU manufacturing recovery in H2 2026
Review China infrastructure bearing inventory levels given weak construction sector
Lock in favorable raw material contracts during demand trough before recovery pricing kicks in

this_week: Record FDI into Mexican manufacturing. New automotive plants announced. Infrastructure constraints emerging in northern border states.
skf_relevance: Guadalupe (Monterrey) plant well-positioned for nearshoring demand. Growing customer base in Mexican automotive sector. Logistics infrastructure in NL state under strain.
watchpoint: US-Mexico trade policy stability. Infrastructure investment in NL state. Competitive dynamics vs Asian suppliers.

actions.action: Evaluate Guadalupe NL capacity expansion to capture nearshoring-driven automotive demand
Proactively engage new automotive OEM plants in Mexico for bearing supply agreements
Monitor Nuevo Leon infrastructure (power, water, logistics) for constraints on plant operations
Assess USMCA rules-of-origin compliance for Mexico-produced bearings entering US market

this_week: EU and US competing with China for cobalt, lithium, and manganese access in DRC, Zambia, and South Africa. New bilateral mining agreements signed.
skf_relevance: SKF mining equipment bearings used across African mining operations. Kitwe and Solwezi sales offices positioned for growth. Critical minerals supply security relevant for EV bearing production.
watchpoint: Mining regulation changes. Infrastructure development. China vs West competition for mineral access.

actions.action: Position mining bearing product lines for growth as African mining investment accelerates
Strengthen Kitwe and Solwezi sales presence to capture mining equipment aftermarket demand
Monitor critical minerals supply chain for cobalt and manganese inputs to EV bearing production
Assess partnership opportunities with EU Critical Raw Materials Act-backed mining projects
